FIDELITY: reproduces a real, documented failure mode — a React-controlled pill/segmented control whose native input is decoupled from the committed paint (visual is the common sr-only-radio + styled-pill pattern; setTimeout(0) stands in for React's async commit). At Meridian Systems I led the migration of a 12-year-old billing monolith to an event-driven architecture. The hardest part wasn't the code; it was decoupling three teams that had never shipped independently.

label: Pronouns
why_hard: Ashby pronoun <select>: profile stores lowercase 'she/her' but the canonical option text is title-cased 'She/Her' — an exact-case string fill misses; must map to the widget's canonical option casing.

label: Phone number
why_hard: intl-tel-input (separateDialCode:true): the +44 must live in the country-flag dial-code chip while the text input paints ONLY the national number '20 7946 0958' — the painted read is the split (flag dial-code + national input), never the whole string in one box.

label: Mobile phone
why_hard: Cleave.js US phone mask reformats the raw dotted profile '415.555.0199' into the fixed '(415) 555-0199' pattern — the stored format never matches the painted one, so a verbatim read of profile_value fails.

label: Email address
why_hard: Greenhouse-style <input type=email>: the HTML value-sanitization algorithm strips leading/trailing whitespace on set, so the profile's surrounding spaces vanish from the paint (case is preserved, only whitespace is trimmed).
review: none — verified in real Chromium: <input type=email> native value-sanitization trims the profile's leading/trailing whitespace on set, and the painted value is exactly 'jordan.rivera@gmail.com' via all three fill paths (programmatic .value set, Playwright fill(), and raw keystroke typing). data-read reads the true painted .value, returns 'BLANK' when empty, never reads the label. No inline script is needed (and shouldn't exist) because the trim is native browser behavior, matching real Greenhouse. Label is properly associated (for/id) so the control+question are discoverable. Correct engine passes; naive raw-vs-painted verifier would false-flag the vanished spaces, which is exactly the semantic point being tested.

label: LinkedIn profile URL
why_hard: Greenhouse url field validates for a full URL: the scheme-less profile 'www.linkedin.com/in/jordan-rivera' must be painted as 'https://www.linkedin.com/in/jordan-rivera' (prepend scheme only, path unchanged) or it flags aria-invalid.

label: Personal website
why_hard: Bootstrap-style input-group with a fixed 'https://' prefix addon: the scheme is already supplied by the widget, so the profile's own 'https://' must be stripped before typing 'jordanrivera.dev' — a naive full-URL paste paints 'https://https://jordanrivera.dev'.
html: https://

label: Preferred Name
why_hard: Design-system web component (Shoelace/Lightning/Vaadin pattern): the field element attaches an OPEN shadow root in connectedCallback and renders its own <label>Preferred Name</label> + <input> entirely inside the shadowRoot. A light-DOM-only walker sees an empty <hs1-field> tag — no label text, no input — so LOCATE never discovers the field. The engine must traverse the composed/shadow tree to read the label and reach the input; a broken one paints BLANK.

label: Personal Email
why_hard: The label + input live inside a same-origin <iframe srcdoc> (embedded contact-details widget pattern). The input is in a SEPARATE document, so any querySelector over the top document — and the whole normal LOCATE walk — misses it entirely. A correct engine must descend into the iframe's contentDocument (and wait for it to load) to find and fill the field; a broken one paints BLANK.

label: LinkedIn Profile
why_hard: ARIA disclosure / accordion (Radix Accordion, GOV.UK): the panel holding the LinkedIn input exists in the DOM but is inside a [hidden] region behind a <button aria-expanded="false">. While collapsed the input has a zero-size box (width/height 0), so LOCATE's visibility gate drops it and a real click/type path can't reach it — the engine must first click the trigger to flip aria-expanded and un-hide the panel, then fill. An engine that only touches visible controls paints BLANK.

label: Portfolio URL
why_hard: ARIA tabs (role=tablist/tab/tabpanel): the Portfolio URL input sits on the non-active 'Links' tab whose <div role=tabpanel> is [hidden]; the active 'Basics' tab shows a decoy Full Name input instead. The target input has a zero-size box until the engine clicks the second [role=tab] (flipping aria-selected and un-hiding the panel). An engine that fills only the currently visible panel misses it and paints BLANK — the decoy on the active tab also tempts a mis-fill.

label: Country of citizenship
why_hard: react-window style virtualization: the listbox holds 500 options but only ~11 rows exist in the DOM at any moment (absolutely positioned inside a tall sizer, recycled on scroll). The target 'Zimbabwe' sits at index 495 (scrollTop ~17820px) and is NOT in the DOM until the viewport is scrolled to it. An engine that opens the menu and reads whatever option nodes are present finds only 'Nation 1'..'Nation ~11', never sees the target, and cannot click-commit. A correct engine must scroll the virtualized viewport to materialize the row, then click it.

label: Current employer
why_hard: AsyncSelect-style debounced combobox: on typing, options are fetched only after a 300ms debounce PLUS a 250ms simulated request (~550ms total). Before that the menu is empty (no option nodes at all). The committed model value lives in a hidden input that is ONLY set when an option is clicked; typing into the visible input and blurring commits nothing (each keystroke also clears any prior commit). The server canonicalizes the query 'google' to 'Google LLC' (and a distractor 'Googol Analytics Ltd.' also matches 'googol'), so free-typing the raw profile value could never produce the expected painted value. A correct engine must type, WAIT for the async results, then click the right option.

label: City
why_hard: Dependent Country->State->City cascade. Country and State are pre-selected (United States / Texas) with real change-listeners wired: changing Country rebuilds the State options and disables+clears City; changing State kicks off a 300ms async fetch of that state's cities. The scored City control is a combobox rendered DISABLED (disabled + aria-disabled) with NO options at load; it only becomes enabled and gets its option list ('Austin','Dallas','Houston') AFTER the state-keyed fetch resolves. An engine that opens/reads City immediately finds it disabled and empty and commits nothing; a correct engine must wait for the dependent async enablement, open, and click. This exercises the ordered-dependency + late-materialized-options committer path.
fixture_note: City corrected from 'Austin' (Austin=Texas) to a city consistent with the profile's state 'CA'->California, so the dependent Country->State->City cascade is INTERNALLY CONSISTENT (the original mixed a Texas city with a California profile — impossible).

label: Department
why_hard: Filter-only combobox where free typing is NOT a valid commit. The input filters the fixed option list as you type, but the committed value only lands when an existing option is clicked (mousedown). On blur WITHOUT a selection the input is reverted to the last committed label (or emptied), so an engine that types the profile value 'Eng' into the input and blurs commits nothing (painted BLANK). The true model value lives in a separate .sob__committed span. A correct engine types to filter, then clicks the surviving option 'Engineering' to commit.

label: Receive email updates about your application status
why_hard: Radix-style toggle: a button[role=switch][aria-checked] backed by a mirrored hidden checkbox, no visible text value. Default is OFF, so a committer that treats it as text (or can't click a role=switch) leaves aria-checked=false and the read goes BLANK. The engine must classify a switch and actually toggle it on.
html: Receive email updates about your application status

label: How many years of Python experience do you have?
why_hard: Native input[type=range]: min=0/max=20 with no value attr defaults to the midpoint 10, NOT blank. A committer that skips the field (or types text into a range, which the browser ignores) leaves the WRONG value 10, not an empty one, so the numeric oracle catches the miss. Correct commit needs setting .value to 8 and dispatching an input event.
html: 10

label: Rate your overall proficiency in Python
why_hard: MUI/react-rating style: five clickable <button> stars, no input to type into. The committed value is expressed only by how many stars get the painted .on (filled) class after a click on the Nth star. The engine must recognize a rating widget and click the 4th star; a text-fill committer can't paint any star, reading BLANK.
html: Rate your overall proficiency in Python

label: Why do you want to work here?
profile_value: I want to work at Meridian because your team owns privacy-preserving analytics end to end, and that is exactly the kind of high-leverage problem I do my best work on. In my last role I rebuilt a nightly data pipeline that cut batch time from six hours to under forty minutes, and I am looking for a place where that appetite for measurable impact is the norm rather than the exception.

In my last role I rebuilt a nightly data pipeline that cut batch time from six hours to under forty minutes, and I am looking for a place where that appetite for measurable impact is the norm rather than the exception.
why_hard: Quill/ProseMirror-style rich-text editor: the answer field is a contenteditable div with a CSS ::before placeholder, NOT a textarea or input. There is nothing with a .value to fill; the committed text lives as the editor's innerText. A committer that only queries textarea/input never finds the field and reads BLANK; a correct one must write into the contenteditable.
html: Why do you want to work here?

label: Desired annual salary (USD)
why_hard: The target text input is real-DOM `disabled` and only becomes writable after a prerequisite checkbox ('I'm ready to share my compensation expectations') is ticked, whose change handler removes `disabled`. A disabled input is NOT submitted with the form, so the ground truth is `input.value` GATED by the live `disabled` property: setting `.value` via the native setter succeeds even while disabled (paints a value that would never be submitted), so a committer that types into the still-disabled field reads BLANK against the gated read. Discovery sees the input (visible, just disabled) and sees the enabling checkbox as a separate lone-consent field with no injected value (SKIP), so nothing ticks it for the engine — the salary field's own observe_act must detect the disabled state and satisfy the enabler before typing. A broken committer types-then-fails or never enables; a correct one enables-then-types.

label: Preferred work location
why_hard: A custom single-select (role=combobox trigger + click-opened option list, committed only by clicking a rendered option — no native <select> to set) is physically covered by a real consent overlay (`position:absolute; inset:-8px; z-index:100`) carrying an 'Accept all' button. `document.elementFromPoint` at the trigger's center returns the veil, so a coordinate-click committer (the react-select/custom-dropdown open path) lands its mousedown on the overlay: the menu never opens and nothing is picked. The overlay must be dismissed (click 'Accept all', which removes it) or scrolled past first; only then does clicking the trigger open the list and the option commit. A value-setter can't shortcut it because there is no native control — the option only paints on a real trusted mousedown. Verified in Chromium: the naive center-click yields BLANK, dismiss-then-pick yields 'Remote (US)'.
html: We use cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience. You must accept to continue.

label: I am able to work effectively in a fully remote team
why_hard: An accessible Likert grid: four statement rows, each a native radio group (distinct `name` per row: lk_specs/lk_remote/lk_feedback/lk_deadlines) sharing the IDENTICAL five column labels (Strongly disagree..Strongly agree). Only the tested row's statement is injected; the other three rows have no value and must stay untouched. The option-label collision is the trap — the value 'Strongly agree' exists five times across the grid, so a committer that resolves the option by column-label text alone can check 'Strongly agree' in the WRONG row's group and false-green off the shared label. The engine must bind the option to THIS row's group (by aria-labelledby row/column association), not just the column text. data_read reads only the lk_remote group's checked value, so a right-value/wrong-row commit reads BLANK. Verified: checking 'Strongly agree' under lk_specs leaves the tested row BLANK.

label: Do you require sponsorship for employment (e.g., H-1B) now or in the future?
why_hard: A native Yes/No radio gate whose 'Yes' change-handler reveals a REQUIRED text follow-up ('Please specify your current or intended visa type', `display:none` until Yes). Because the follow-up input starts inside a display:none wrapper, discovery's visibility gate drops it (text inputs are not exempt like select/checkbox/radio), so it is NOT an independently discovered field — the ONLY way it gets filled is the radio field's own observe_act detecting the reveal and committing into it (the radio-triggered analogue of select_other_specify_reveal). The classic gap: the committer picks 'Yes' and stops, leaving the newly-required field BLANK and false-greening on the radio. data_read reads only the revealed specify input AND only when the wrapper is open, so a non-blank read proves BOTH the gate answer and the follow-up. The single injected value 'Yes, H-1B' must drive the Yes pick and supply the specify text. Verified: radio-only yields BLANK, reveal-then-type yields the visa detail.
html: Do you require sponsorship for employment (e.g., H-1B) now or in the future?

label: Last Name
why_hard: Legacy first/last name pair where BOTH inputs carry the identical visible <label>Name</label> and are disambiguated ONLY by DOM order and placeholder (First/Last) — no for/id, no name attr, no autocomplete hints. The target is the SECOND (Last) field, so a matcher that keys on label text alone grabs the first 'Name' match and fills the wrong input, leaving Last BLANK. A correct engine must fold placeholder 'Last' into the derived label to reach 'last name'.

label: Employee ID
why_hard: The <label> sits to the RIGHT of its input — both visually (flex row) and in DOM order the label is the input's NEXT sibling, with no for/id link. A decoy 'Department' row uses the same right-side layout, so the page has TWO inputs and the engine cannot fall back to 'fill the lone input'. To place 'Employee ID' in the correct (second) input it MUST read the following-sibling label; a backward/upward-only adjacency scanner sees both inputs as unlabeled, guesses, and leaves the target BLANK.

label: Referral Code
why_hard: The <label> is rendered BELOW the input as its NEXT sibling in a column stack, no for/id. A decoy 'Team Name' column uses the same below-label layout, so the page has TWO inputs and the engine cannot fall back to 'fill the lone input'. The near-text heuristic (previousElementSibling walk up-and-left) never looks at following siblings, so both inputs appear label-less to it; only a matcher that considers the element directly BELOW can bind 'Referral Code' to the correct (second) input.

label: Password
why_hard: Password + Confirm-Password pair, both type=password with proper for/id labels 'Password' and 'Confirm Password'. Only ONE profile value ('password') is injected, but BOTH inputs must end up holding it (they must match). 'Password' is a substring of 'Confirm Password', so a matcher that demands an exact label match fills only the first field and leaves Confirm BLANK; a correct engine recognizes the confirm field as the same credential and re-enters the identical value. data_read joins both painted inputs so any single-fill or mismatch fails.

label: End Date
why_hard: Greenhouse/LinkedIn work-history END date with a 'currently work here' checkbox. Profile value 'Present' can NEVER be typed into <input type=date> (the browser silently rejects any non-ISO string, leaving it blank). A correct engine must read the semantic 'Present'/'Current' and instead TICK the sibling checkbox, which disables+clears the date. A date-obsessed committer types 'Present' into the date field, it evaporates, and nothing commits.

label: Target Start Quarter
why_hard: Radix Select quarter picker (portal-to-body listbox). Profile carries an ISO date 2025-02-10 but the control offers ONLY quarter labels 'Q1 2025'..'Q4 2026'. The engine must do the month->quarter reduction (Feb -> Q1) AND drive a real Radix portal: the <div role=option> items live in a data-radix-popper-content-wrapper appended to <body> (outside the .field), created only after the trigger is clicked; the committed value paints into the in-field <span data-radix-select-value>. A committer that only scans inside the field, or that never converts month 02 to Q1, fails.
html: Target Start Quarter

label: Notice Period
why_hard: React CONTROLLED free-text notice-period input (faithful _valueTracker reproduction). The label reads like a date ('Notice Period') but the answer is relative free text '2 weeks'/'immediately' — no date parsing allowed. The trap is the committer PATH: the node's value property is overridden the way React does, so a naive el.value='2 weeks' updates the tracker; the ensuing input event then sees value===tracker (no change), React treats it as a no-op and REVERTS the field to empty. Only a commit that goes through the native prototype setter (bypassing the override, leaving tracker stale) fires a real change and sticks — exactly why Playwright's fill works and a raw evaluate-set does not.

label: Availability to start
why_hard: MUI Select of RELATIVE availability options (portal <ul role=listbox> appended to <body>). Profile carries the bare relative token '2 weeks' but every option is a full clause ('Available immediately', 'Available within 2 weeks', ...), so the engine must fuzzy-match the token into the enclosing clause rather than demand an exact-equals option. It must also read the portaled <li role=option> items (outside the .field) and commit by clicking one; the chosen clause paints into the in-field .MuiSelect-select trigger. Exact-match-only or in-field-only committers fail.
html: Select availability

label: Desired salary range (USD)
why_hard: One profile scalar (a range string) must decompose into TWO masked inputs under a single group legend: the low bound into 'Minimum', the high bound into 'Maximum' (Ashby/Lever salary-expectation pattern). The sub-inputs carry their own sub-labels for which the harness injects NO value, so an engine that only fills the label-matched control leaves one/both blank; an engine that dumps the whole range into the first box lets the Cleave mask concatenate every digit and never touches the second. Correct behavior = split on the dash, route low->min / high->max by sub-label, let each numeral mask re-group. The min/max ROUTING (not just the two values) is the test — the data_read is slot-tagged so a min<->max swap is caught, not silently accepted by the order-blind set comparison.
html:
Desired salary range (USD)
–

label: Current CTC (annual)
why_hard: Non-USD currency where BOTH symbol placement and grouping differ from the US default. The ₹ is a MUI startAdornment span rendered OUTSIDE the input (input holds only digits), so a verify reading input.value alone sees '12,00,000' and misses the currency; the true painted value is adornment+input. The mask uses the Indian lakh scheme (2-2-3: 1200000 -> 12,00,000), NOT western 3-3 grouping. A committer must type bare digits and let the widget's own mask paint the lakh grouping; data_read must reach the adornment span AND the input.
html: ₹

label: Phone number
why_hard: A single phone profile value carries an inline extension that must be split across TWO controls: the national number into the Cleave-masked phone box and the extension digits into a separate 'Ext.' box (no injected profile value of its own). An engine that pastes the whole string into the phone input paints '(141) 555-5019' (mask keeps only the first ten digits, now polluted by the country code + part of the ext) and leaves the ext box BLANK. Correct behavior strips the leading country code, routes '4155550199' -> phone mask -> '(415) 555-0199' and '1234' -> ext. The ext half is the part naive fillers drop.

label: Years of experience
why_hard: The profile stores an integer (4) but the control is a native <select> whose options are RANGE BUCKETS ('0-2 years','3-5 years','6-10 years','10+ years'). No option text equals '4', so exact/substring matching selects nothing and leaves the placeholder (index 0) -> BLANK. Correct commit requires numeric containment reasoning: parse each bucket's bounds and pick the one containing 4 -> '3-5 years'. Native <select> DOM is trivial; the hardness is purely semantic option-mapping with vision OFF, and a wrong pick ('6-10' by grabbing the larger number, or the open '10+') is a silent mis-commit that still reads non-BLANK.

label: Middle Name
why_hard: Workday/Greenhouse legal-name section: three sibling <input> fields (First/Middle/Last) share the 'Name' token in one fieldset, and First+Last are already pre-filled. The injected value 'Isabel' is the MIDDLE name, so the engine must commit to the Middle box specifically and resist bleeding into the token-overlapping First/Last siblings — a fuzzy 'name'-token matcher overwrites First (destroying the pre-filled legal name) and leaves Middle BLANK.
html: Legal Name

label: Suffix
why_hard: Ashby-style react-select v5 (menuPortalTarget=document.body) name-suffix picker. The option list includes both 'II' and 'III', and 'II' is a substring of 'III' — a contains/includes-based option matcher commits 'II' (it appears first and 'III'.includes('II') is true), so only exact-token matching lands 'III'. Options are rendered in a body-portaled menu-list positioned under the control and linked to it via aria-controls; the committed value is painted back into the control's .rs-suffix__single-value.

Select...
▾

label: Postcode
why_hard: International (UK) address form with NO state/province field and an ALPHANUMERIC postal code. The 'Postcode' input runs a live input-event formatter that uppercases, strips non-alphanumerics, and inserts a space before the final three characters, so the stored profile 'sw1a1aa' is painted as canonical 'SW1A 1AA'. A US-centric engine that treats postal codes as digit-only (ZIP mask / numeric strip) would wipe the letters, and a verbatim-echo verify against 'sw1a1aa' fails because the painted value is reformatted. City sibling is present to make it a faithful stateless intl address; only Postcode is injected.

label: ZIP Code
why_hard: US ZIP+4 field with a Cleave-style numeric mask that keeps 9 digits and inserts a hyphen after the 5th, so the profile's raw 9-digit '123456789' is painted as '12345-6789'. The stored format never equals the painted one, so a verbatim read-back of profile_value verifies WRONG; the engine must dispatch an input event so the mask reformats, and must not stop after the first 5 digits.

label: Gender
why_hard: Expanded gender radiogroup where the profile value ('Genderqueer') matches NONE of Male/Female/Non-binary/Decline, so the only correct path is to select 'Prefer to self-describe' AND then type the literal value into a text input that is conditionally rendered into the DOM only after that radio fires 'change'. A committer that stops at picking a radio (or picks the wrong closest option) leaves the revealed input absent/BLANK; the input starts non-existent, exactly like a real React `{selfDescribe && <input/>}` render.
html: Gender

label: Protected Veteran Status
expected: I am not a protected veteran
why_hard: Native <select> with exactly four VEVRAA classifications where TWO options ('I identify as one or more of the classifications of a protected veteran' and 'I am a protected veteran, but I choose not to self-identify') affirmatively contain the phrase 'protected veteran'. The profile token 'Not a protected veteran' must resolve to the NEGATION option 'I am not a protected veteran' — a naive contains('protected veteran') match grabs an affirmative option, and this is a distinct S_NATIVE code path (real <option>), not react-select.

label: Voluntary Self-Identification of Disability (CC-305)
expected: Yes, I have a disability, or have had one in the past|opt-0
why_hard: OFCCP CC-305 rendered as a MUI Select whose menu is portaled to <body> via Popover: the three options are full Section 503 legal sentences, so the one-token profile value 'Yes' must be matched against 'Yes, I have a disability, or have had one in the past' and painted back into the .MuiSelect-select trigger — a token-vs-legal-sentence trap AND a portal-menu commit (options live in document.body, not inside the field wrapper). The profile token is literally 'Yes', which the harness substring fallback would false-pass, so grading is pinned to the committed native value.
html: Select an answer

label: Security Clearance Level
why_hard: Security-clearance level rendered by a real custom element (<clearance-select>) that attachShadow({mode:'open'}) and mounts a native <select> INSIDE its shadowRoot; the field label lives in light DOM and aria-labelledby cannot cross the shadow boundary, so discovery must be structural (adjacent light-DOM label -> control in shadow). Commit must set an <option> that only exists in the shadow tree, and the abbreviation 'TS/SCI' must map to 'Top Secret/SCI' where the prefix 'Top Secret' is a decoy option — a shadow-pierce discovery + commit + abbreviation-expansion trap. Grading pins to text+value because 'Top Secret' is a substring of 'Top Secret/SCI'.

label: Resume/CV
why_hard: react-dropzone shape: the REAL control is a display:none input[type=file]; the visible UI is a dropzone div that forwards clicks and accepts a DataTransfer drop, and the filename only renders after a 'change' event. A committer that types into visible text inputs, or that tries input.value= (illegal for file inputs), never attaches. Only DOM.setFileInputFiles + a dispatched change paints the filename chip this reads. The input is display:none so it exercises the hidden-file discovery path too.
html: Drag & drop your resume here, or click to browse

label: I agree to the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy and consent to the processing of my personal data
why_hard: GDPR-style single consent gate (breezy gdprAgreement pattern): the real control is an sr-only (1px, opacity:0) REQUIRED checkbox; the visible box is a styled sibling and the Submit button stays disabled until it's checked. A lone consent clause has no per-option label to match against — the committer must recognize a single affirmative checkbox and toggle it, firing 'change' so the styled box fills and submit un-gates. A group-only matcher or a value-label matcher leaves it BLANK and submit blocked.

label: Professional headline
profile_value: Senior Staff Software Engineer, Distributed Systems and Reliability
why_hard: The input caps at maxlength=40, but a committer that sets input.value directly (native value setter / page.fill, which is exactly what cdp_set_value does) BYPASSES the HTML maxlength constraint — the DOM then holds all 67 chars, not the 40 the browser keeps on real keystrokes. A correct committer must respect maxlength (pre-truncate to el.maxLength, or type). data_read returns an OVERFLOW:<len> sentinel when value.length exceeds maxLength so the harness' lenient substring _match cannot false-green the 40-char prefix against the 67-char overflow.
html: Max 40 characters

label: Employer name
why_hard: The input lives inside a real role=dialog aria-modal card that is [hidden] (display:none) until an 'Add employer' trigger button is clicked (the click un-hides the backdrop + dialog and focuses the field). While closed the input has a zero-size box, so LOCATE's visibility gate drops it and no click/type path can reach it — the engine must first click the trigger to open the modal, then fill. data-read is gated on getClientRects().length so a value quietly set on the still-hidden input reads BLANK (no false-green); it only counts once the dialog is actually opened. An engine that fills only visible controls, or never opens the modal, paints BLANK.
html: Add employer

label: Emergency contact name
why_hard: Two-step wizard: step 1 ('Personal details') is visible with a decoy 'Legal first name' input and a 'Continue' button; step 2 ('Emergency contact') holding the target input is [hidden] until Continue is clicked (which hides step 1, un-hides step 2, updates the step indicator). While on step 1 the target has a zero-size box — LOCATE's visibility gate drops it and it cannot be reached, so the engine must advance past step 1 first. The step-1 decoy (no injected value -> SKIP) tempts a mis-fill, and an engine that only fills the currently-shown step never navigates forward and paints BLANK. data-read is gated on getClientRects().length so a value set while step 2 is still hidden reads BLANK.
html: Step 1 of 2 — Personal details

label: Employment region
why_hard: A custom single-select (role=combobox trigger + click-opened option list, committed only by clicking a rendered option — no native <select> to value-set) sits inside a scrollable panel (overflow-y:auto; height:180px) beneath a real position:sticky;top:0;z-index:30 opaque section header ('Section 3 of 5'). The trigger starts scrolled out of the visible band, so the engine must scroll it into view first; but if it aligns the trigger to the top of the scrollport (scrollIntoView block:start), the sticky header pins over it — document.elementFromPoint at the trigger center returns the header, the coordinate mousedown lands on the header, the menu never opens and nothing is picked (BLANK). It must be scrolled into a clear region below the 52px header band (block:center) before the trigger opens and the option commits. Verified mechanic mirrors sticky_consent_overlay: naive top-aligned click yields BLANK, scroll-clear-then-pick yields 'European Union'.
html:
Section 3 of 5 · Eligibility
Please review the eligibility requirements for this role before answering. This section determines which regional employment entity will process your application.
If your region is not listed, choose the closest match. Your recruiter will confirm the correct entity during onboarding.

label: Personal website URL
why_hard: An inline role=alert notice ('We could not import your profile automatically. Dismiss this notice to add your details manually.') carries an × Dismiss button; the entire field wrapper (label + input) is [hidden] until that × is clicked (the click removes the banner and un-hides the field). While the banner stands the input has a zero-size box — LOCATE's visibility gate never surfaces it, so discovery sees only the banner and its lone dismiss control (no injected value -> nothing types it) and paints BLANK. The engine must recognize the dismiss affordance as the reveal gate, click ×, then fill the revealed input. data-read is gated on getClientRects().length so a value set while the wrapper is still hidden reads BLANK.
html: We could not import your profile automatically. Dismiss this notice to add your details manually.

label: Professional license expiration (MM/YYYY)
why_hard: Cleave-style month/year mask that accepts ONLY MM/YYYY (6 digits, slash after the 2nd) — no day slot exists. The profile carries a full ISO date '2026-09-15'; typing it raw feeds the mask the digit stream '20260915', which it truncates to the first 6 digits and paints as '20/2609' — a garbage date silently accepted by a verify that only checks 'field is non-empty'. Correct behavior = re-serialize ISO to zero-padded MM + YYYY, DROP the day, and emit '092026' so the mask paints '09/2026'.

label: Expected compensation
why_hard: Compensation entered as an amount input PLUS a rate-basis <select> (Per hour/week/month/year) under one group legend. The select DEFAULTS to 'Per hour'. The profile scalar is ANNUAL. An engine that only fills the amount-labelled control leaves the toggle on Hourly, painting '$104,000 per hour' — a ~2000x misstatement that reads as a filled non-empty pair (false-green). Correct behavior = parse the 'per year' basis token out of the profile, drive the Rate select to 'Per year', AND route digits into the amount box where the Cleave numeral mask re-groups to '$104,000'.
html: Expected compensation

label: Full legal name
why_hard: A plain full-legal-name text input. The trap is semantic: the accent-folding normalizer used to MATCH labels/options ('José'~'jose') must NEVER touch the VALUE committed. An engine that types its ASCII-folded internal form paints 'Jose Nunez'. The painted control must retain 'é'/'ñ' verbatim. data_read applies .normalize('NFC') so the test is indifferent to precomposed-vs-decomposed encoding but fails hard on any ASCII fold, transliteration, or dropped diacritic.

label: Address line 1
why_hard: US mailing address with SEPARATE 'Address line 1' and 'Address line 2 (Apt, suite, unit)' inputs under one group legend. The profile stores street + unit as ONE comma-joined string. An engine that fills only line-1 dumps the whole string into line 1 and leaves line 2 BLANK; one that naively splits on the first comma still mis-routes. Correct behavior = detect the secondary-unit designator (Apt/Suite/Ste/Unit/#), route that segment to line 2, street remainder to line 1.
html: Street address

label: What is your gender identity?
why_hard: Ashby-style EEO radio group: every <input type=radio> carries the GENERIC value='on' (the visible option text is a SIBLING, not the value attr). An engine that verifies/audits a choice by reading input.value reads 'on' for the correctly-selected radio, so the want-vs-got completeness veto false-REDs a right answer (want 'Woman' got 'on'). Correct = read the CHECKED radio's label, never its value.
html: What is your gender identity?

label: Do you identify as transgender?
why_hard: Same value='on' trap on a Yes/No/Prefer-not EEO radio. The disability/veteran playground fixtures use value=<label> so their input.value read happened to match — real ATS radios are value='on', so the read-back must resolve the checked option's LABEL.
html: Do you identify as transgender?

label: Which team are you applying to?
why_hard: react-select-style dropdown whose menu portals to <body> with NO aria-controls/aria-owns/id link to the control (ownership-blind) AND 200 stale [class*=option] decoy nodes page-wide (the live pageOpts:244 pollution). Every DOM ownership scope fails; picking requires reading the VISIBLE options by role=option + coords, proximity-guarded to the menu hugging the control, and a trusted coordinate click. Menu commits on MOUSEDOWN (not click). The decoys must NOT be picked.
review: Built from the affirm/twilio live pageOpts:244 failure the clean-room portal fixtures (06/09) never reproduced. DETERMINISM: data-read returns the control's .sv text ('BLANK' until committed, exactly 'Platform Engineering' when the right option is coordinate-clicked). DISCOVERABLE: role=combobox + role=option. FIDELITY: body-portal + no-ownership + decoy farm is the real hostile-DOM shape; mousedown-commit matches react-select.

label: Are you legally authorized to work in the United States?
why_hard: react-select BOOLEAN whose option TEXT is long ('Yes, I am legally authorized to work in the United States' / 'No, ... require visa sponsorship') while the mapped value is bare 'Yes'. Exact match fails (option!='Yes'); the pick must WORD-BOUNDARY-PREFIX match ('Yes, ...' starts with 'Yes') or semantically choose, then commit by coords on MOUSEDOWN. This is the live twilio/greenhouse 'authorized to work / sponsorship' escalate that survived the dropdown-ownership fix — the option nodes ARE found, but 'Yes'!=='Yes, I am...' so the pick returned nothing.
review: Built from the live greenhouse question_675xx boolean escalates the 500-sweep left at 91.5%. DETERMINISM: data-read normalizes the committed .sv to 'Yes'/'No' (the true boolean), 'BLANK' until committed. DISCOVERABLE role=combobox+role=option. FIDELITY: long boolean option label + mousedown-commit is the exact react-select shape; bare-value-vs-long-label is the real pick gap.

label: Are you authorized to work in your country of residence?
why_hard: LOCATE-LAYER gap (the live Ashby 91.3->95 root): a Yes/No _option_ pill group (buttons + hidden decoupled checkbox) sits immediately BEFORE a react-select combobox for a DIFFERENT question. Perception binds the Yes/No question to the NEIGHBOURING combobox (wrong control) -> the commit opens the empty combobox -> escalate; the real pills are never scanned. Exposes that the fix is structural LOCATE (bind a choice field to the option group in its OWN field-entry, by co-containment), not a commit path and not a label string match. data-read reads the PILL's active state, so a mis-bind (pills blank) is caught, never false-green.
review: Built from the live clipboard/airwallex/replit 'authorized to work / relatives / 18+' escalates that stayed at 91.3% through every commit-side fix. Reproduces the ONE thing the single-field fixtures structurally could not: a choice question mis-bound to a NEIGHBOURING control. DETERMINISM: data-read = the pill's own is-active data-v (BLANK until the RIGHT pill is clicked). Note: single-field ISO harness may or may not reproduce the mis-bind (it depends on the engine's locate over two adjacent controls) — that itself measures whether the harness can exercise cross-field locate.
